2604.08494.title: What They Saw, Not Just Where They Looked: Semantic Scanpath Similarity via VLMs and NLP metric
2604.08494.paper_summary: Scanpath similarity metrics are central to eye-movement research, yet existing methods predominantly evaluate spatial and temporal alignment while neglecting semantic equivalence between attended image regions. We present a semantic scanpath similarity framework that integrates vision-language models (VLMs) into eye-tracking analysis. Each fixation is encoded under controlled visual context (patch-based and marker-based strategies) and transformed into concise textual descriptions, which are aggregated into scanpath-level representations. Semantic similarity is then computed using embedding-based and lexical NLP metrics and compared against established spatial measures, including MultiMatch and DTW. Experiments on free-viewing eye-tracking data demonstrate that semantic similarity captures partially independent variance from geometric alignment, revealing cases of high content agreement despite spatial divergence. We further analyze the impact of contextual encoding on description fidelity and metric stability. Our findings suggest that multimodal foundation models enable interpretable, content-aware extensions of classical scanpath analysis, providing a complementary dimension for gaze research within the ETRA community.
2604.08494.paper_summary_zh: 掃描路徑相似性指標在眼動研究中至關重要，但現有方法主要評估空間和時間對齊，而忽略了注意圖像區域之間的語義等價性。我們提出了一個語義掃描路徑相似性框架，將視覺-語言模型（VLMs）整合到眼動追蹤分析中。每個注視在受控的視覺上下文下進行編碼（基於區塊和基於標記的策略），並轉換為簡潔的文本描述，這些描述被聚合成掃描路徑級別的表示。然後使用基於嵌入和詞彙的自然語言處理指標計算語義相似性，並與已建立的空間測量進行比較，包括MultiMatch和DTW。在自由觀看眼動追蹤數據上的實驗表明，語義相似性捕捉到來自幾何對齊的部分獨立變異，揭示了儘管空間上存在差異，內容仍高度一致的情況。我們進一步分析了上下文編碼對描述真實性和指標穩定性的影響。我們的研究結果表明，多模態基礎模型使得經典掃描路徑分析的可解釋性和內容感知擴展成為可能，為ETRA社群中的凝視研究提供了一個互補的維度。
2604.08494.author: Mohamed Amine Kerkouri et.al.
2604.08494.authors: Mohamed Amine Kerkouri, Marouane Tliba, Bin Wang, Aladine Chetouani, Ulas Bagci, Alessandro Bruno
